raw_response:
{
  "communicative_function": "suggestive prescription of a desired behavioral pattern via positive autosuggestion",
  "implicit_claims": [
    "hunger occurs reliably three times daily at conventional meal times",
    "hunger is inherently an agreeable/pleasant sensation",
    "enjoyment of food and absence of overeating will follow automatically from this hunger experience",
    "the speaker's statement can causally produce these outcomes"
  ],
  "coherence_assessment": "internally consistent as a prescriptive script, though it equates hunger with pleasure and assumes uniform appetite regulation without addressing variability",
  "conditions_for_usefulness": "recipient is receptive to autosuggestion, seeks normalized appetite cues, has no underlying metabolic or psychological conditions affecting hunger, and applies the statement within a supportive therapeutic or self-help framework",
  "conditions_for_misleading": "presented as an empirical prediction or guaranteed outcome rather than suggestion, or applied to individuals with disordered eating, medical appetite dysregulation, or cultural/physiological differences in meal timing"
}
